Mother should initiate breast-feeding as early as possible after delivery and feed the child on demand. Milk production of the mother is determined by the infant’s demand. Frequent sucking by the baby and complete emptying of breast are important for sustaining adequate breast milk output. A working mother can express her breast milk and store it hygienically up to 8 hrs. This can be fed to her infant by the caretaker. Since, drugs (antibiotics, caffeine, hormones and alcohol) are secreted into the breast-milk and could prove harmful to the breast-fed infant, caution should be exercised by the lactating mother while taking medicines. 27 Should HIV positive women breast feed their
